## Task
Found in PM review 2026-07-10 (verify-not-trust pass over PRs #98-#112): STORE-001 closed as done but shipped PARTIAL. The schema module (src/schema, combinator validator, inferred types) and the config migration are real and good; but the packet's core requirements were narrowed silently:
- 14 raw JSON.parse sites remain in production code outside src/schema: src/db/backup.ts (5), src/cli/commands/task.ts (3), src/adopt/* (3), src/cli/commands/handoff.ts (1), src/packets/document.ts (1), src/tasks/service.ts (1). The packet demanded migrating all of them.
- The eslint JSON.parse ban was scoped to ONLY two files (src/config.ts, src/db/store.ts) instead of all of src/ minus src/schema — so the rail cannot catch new raw sites, which is the entire point (STORE-001 stop condition: "leaving any JSON.parse outside src/schema").
Finish the class:
1. Migrate the 14 sites through src/schema parse (define row/payload schemas where missing: backup metadata, task --json inputs, handoff state, packet frontmatter JSON fields, adopt inventories).
2. Widen the eslint no-restricted-syntax JSON.parse ban to all TS production code except src/schema/** (test files may keep it for fixtures). Zero disables.
3. The widened lint IS the enforcement — verify fails on any new raw site forever.
Root cause to record (review process): the reviewer approved without grepping the stop conditions literally. Rubric note: stop conditions are grep-checkable claims; the reviewer runs the grep.

## RED test (write first)
The failing check is the widened lint itself: widen the eslint rule FIRST, run npm run lint, and capture the ~14 violations as the red evidence. Then migrate sites until lint is green. No new test name needed beyond existing schema round-trip tests extended to the new boundaries (backup metadata + task --json at minimum).
Expected failure cause (literal string in the output): the eslint message "JSON.parse is forbidden outside src/schema/. Use schema validation instead." reported on the unmigrated files.

## Reuse
src/schema core combinators (STORE-001's module — extend, do not fork); the existing config migration as the pattern; the eslint rule text already present.

## Stop conditions
eslint-disable comments as migration (defeats the rail); schemas so loose they validate nothing (every field constrained); a second validator; touching files outside the write_set.

## Evidence required at close
red-test-output, verify-root, final-sha.
